Historical texts indicate that in the 5th year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Emperor Jitō}}'s reign (691 CE), an imperial order was issued to enshrine "Mizuchi-no-Kami" in Shinano Province.<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇5th year 8月23日条。</ref> This is the first recorded mention of the shrine. While the Engishiki does not list "Mizuchi-no-Kami," Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine is the only major shrine in Mizuchi District, leading scholars to identify it as Mizuchi-no-Kami.
== Deity == The shrine is believed to enshrine "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake no Kami," but interpretations of the deity vary among the three candidate shrines: * Identical to {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} ** This theory equates the enshrined deity with Takeminakata no Mikoto, the deity of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha}}. * A child of Takeminakata no Mikoto ** This theory suggests the name combines "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto" with "Hikokamiwake," indicating the deity is a child of Takeminakata no Mikoto.<ref name=kamigami/> In Suwa Taisha, the deity is worshipped as {{ill|Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|qq=}} at the Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary of the Lower Shrine. * Two deities ** This theory posits that the name "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake no Kami" refers to two distinct deities: Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto, identified with Takeminakata no Mikoto, and Hikokamiwake no Kami, seen as his child (e.g., {{ill|Tsumashina Shrine|ja|妻科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumashina Shrine|qq=}}).
== Identification == 1. The shrine stands on a hill east of {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkō-ji}} and was within its precincts until 1878, known as "Toshigami-dō Hachiman Shrine." The 1356 text {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|en|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|WD=Q11631818|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba}} identifies Toshigami-dō as a branch of Suwa Taisha and Mizuchi-no-Kami, mentioned in the 691 CE records.<ref>『諏方大明神画詞』抜粋。（『長野県の地名』健御名方富命彦神別神社項より）。</ref> Edo-period documents like the {{ill|Imoi Sanpōki|ja|芋井三宝記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imoi Sanpōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} also assert this identification.<ref>『日本歴史地名体系 長野県の地名』（{{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}）長野市 健御名方富命彦神別神社項。</ref> The site, historically associated with the {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏|1=Kanasashi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646498}} and central to Mizuchi District, offers a strategic view of Zenkōji Plain, supporting its identification as the shrine's original location.<ref name=kamigami>『日本の神々』健御名方富命彦神別神社（長野市長野）項。</ref>
2. Located in the present-day Mizuchi district, this shrine is still referred to as "Mizuchi Shrine" and enshrines Hikokamiwake no Mikoto, considered a child of Takeminakata no Mikoto.
3. Situated on the edge of the Iiyama Basin, traditionally believed to have been a lake called "Mizuchi Sea," this shrine includes unique deities such as {{ill|Niwa-tsume no Mikoto|ja|庭津女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwa-tsume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579706}} and {{ill|Sana Tsubura Hime no Mikoto|ja|沙南豆良姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Tsubura Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578885}}, reflecting its connection to local ruling clans.

{{Coordinates list}} '''Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine''' (健御名方富命彦神別神社) is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Myōjin Taisha) in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}, {{ill|Mizuchi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuchi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} mentions the shrine in Shinano Province, Mizuchi District, as "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Myōjin Taisha)." However, its precise location has since been lost. Three shrines are currently considered candidates for its identification, though no consensus has been reached: * 1. {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Hako Shimizu)|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Hako Shimizu)|qq=}} (Hako Shimizu, Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture: {{Coord|36|39|41.54|N|138|11|31.71|E|region:JP-20|name=Hako Shimizu Shrine}}) - Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, also known as "Mizuchi Taisha" or "Jōyama Kensha." * 2. {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Shinshū Shinmachi)|ja|健御名方冨命彦神別神社 (長野市信州新町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Shinshū Shinmachi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shinshū Shinmachi, Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture: {{Coord|36|34|31.29|N|138|02|56.30|E|region:JP-20|name=Shinshū Shrine}}) - Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, also referred to as "Mizuchi Shrine" or "Mizuchi Taisha." * 3. {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Iiyama City)|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社 (飯山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Iiyama City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iiyama City, Nagano Prefecture: {{Coord|36|55|15.85|N|138|22|29.45|E|region:JP-20|name=Iiyama Shrine}}) - Former Ken-sha, also known as "Itsuka Shrine."
Historical texts indicate that in the 5th year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Emperor Jitō}}'s reign (691 CE), an imperial order was issued to enshrine "Mizuchi-no-Kami" in Shinano Province.<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇5th year 8月23日条。</ref> This is the first recorded mention of the shrine. == Identification == 1. The shrine stands on a hill east of {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkō-ji}} and was within its precincts until 1878, known as "Toshigami-dō Hachiman Shrine." The 1356 text {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|en|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|WD=Q11631818|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba}} identifies Toshigami-dō as a branch of Suwa Taisha and Mizuchi-no-Kami, mentioned in the 691 CE records.<ref>『諏方大明神画詞』抜粋。（『長野県の地名』健御名方富命彦神別神社項より）。</ref> Edo-period documents like the {{ill|Imoi Sanpōki|ja|芋井三宝記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imoi Sanpōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} also assert this identification.<ref>『日本歴史地名体系 長野県の地名』（{{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}）長野市 健御名方富命彦神別神社項。</ref> The site, historically associated with the {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏|1=Kanasashi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646498}} and central to Mizuchi District, offers a strategic view of Zenkōji Plain, supporting its identification as the shrine's original location.<ref name=kamigami>『日本の神々』健御名方富命彦神別神社（長野市長野）項。</ref>

Historical texts indicate that in the 5th year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Emperor Jitō}}'s reign (691 CE), an imperial order was issued to enshrine "Mizuchi-no-Kami" in Shinano Province.<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇5th year 8月23日条。</ref> This is the first recorded mention of the shrine. While the Engishiki does not list "Mizuchi-no-Kami," Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine is the only major shrine in Mizuchi District, leading scholars to identify it as Mizuchi-no-Kami.
== Deity == The shrine is believed to enshrine "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake no Kami," but interpretations of the deity vary among the three candidate shrines: * Identical to {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} ** This theory equates the enshrined deity with Takeminakata no Mikoto, the deity of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha}}. * A child of Takeminakata no Mikoto ** This theory suggests the name combines "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto" with "Hikokamiwake," indicating the deity is a child of Takeminakata no Mikoto.<ref name=kamigami/> In Suwa Taisha, the deity is worshipped as {{ill|Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|qq=}} at the Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary of the Lower Shrine. * Two deities ** This theory posits that the name "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake no Kami" refers to two distinct deities: Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto, identified with Takeminakata no Mikoto, and Hikokamiwake no Kami, seen as his child (e.g., {{ill|Tsumashina Shrine|ja|妻科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumashina Shrine|qq=}}).
== Identification == 1. The shrine stands on a hill east of {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkō-ji}} and was within its precincts until 1878, known as "Toshigami-dō Hachiman Shrine." The 1356 text {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|en|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|WD=Q11631818|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba}} identifies Toshigami-dō as a branch of Suwa Taisha and Mizuchi-no-Kami, mentioned in the 691 CE records.<ref>『諏方大明神画詞』抜粋。（『長野県の地名』健御名方富命彦神別神社項より）。</ref> Edo-period documents like the {{ill|Imoi Sanpōki|ja|芋井三宝記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imoi Sanpōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} also assert this identification.<ref>『日本歴史地名体系 長野県の地名』（{{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}）長野市 健御名方富命彦神別神社項。</ref> The site, historically associated with the {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏|1=Kanasashi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646498}} and central to Mizuchi District, offers a strategic view of Zenkōji Plain, supporting its identification as the shrine's original location.<ref name=kamigami>『日本の神々』健御名方富命彦神別神社（長野市長野）項。</ref>
2. Located in the present-day Mizuchi district, this shrine is still referred to as "Mizuchi Shrine" and enshrines Hikokamiwake no Mikoto, considered a child of Takeminakata no Mikoto.
3. Situated on the edge of the Iiyama Basin, traditionally believed to have been a lake called "Mizuchi Sea," this shrine includes unique deities such as {{ill|Niwa-tsume no Mikoto|ja|庭津女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwa-tsume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579706}} and {{ill|Sana Tsubura Hime no Mikoto|ja|沙南豆良姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Tsubura Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578885}}, reflecting its connection to local ruling clans.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Keichirō Kobayashi, "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City)," Takeo Saitō, "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Iiyama City Itsuka Shrine)" in {{ill|Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites|ja|日本の神々 -神社と聖地-|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 9 (Mino, Hida, Shinano), {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}.
== External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/01hansui/takeminato.htm Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Iiyama City)] (Nagano Prefecture Shrine Office) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/246001.html Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Archives")

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:健御名方富命彦神別神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:健御名方富命彦神別神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:健御名方富命彦神別神社]]
{{Coordinates list}} '''Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine''' (健御名方富命彦神別神社) is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Myōjin Taisha) in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}, {{ill|Mizuchi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuchi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} mentions the shrine in Shinano Province, Mizuchi District, as "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Myōjin Taisha)." However, its precise location has since been lost. Three shrines are currently considered candidates for its identification, though no consensus has been reached: * 1. {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Hako Shimizu)|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Hako Shimizu)|qq=}} (Hako Shimizu, Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture: {{Coord|36|39|41.54|N|138|11|31.71|E|region:JP-20|name=Hako Shimizu Shrine}}) - Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, also known as "Mizuchi Taisha" or "Jōyama Kensha." * 2. {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Shinshū Shinmachi)|ja|健御名方冨命彦神別神社 (長野市信州新町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Shinshū Shinmachi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shinshū Shinmachi, Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture: {{Coord|36|34|31.29|N|138|02|56.30|E|region:JP-20|name=Shinshū Shrine}}) - Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, also referred to as "Mizuchi Shrine" or "Mizuchi Taisha." * 3. {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Iiyama City)|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社 (飯山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Iiyama City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iiyama City, Nagano Prefecture: {{Coord|36|55|15.85|N|138|22|29.45|E|region:JP-20|name=Iiyama Shrine}}) - Former Ken-sha, also known as "Itsuka Shrine."
Historical texts indicate that in the 5th year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Emperor Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}'s reign (691 CE), an imperial order was issued to enshrine "Mizuchi-no-Kami" in Shinano Province.<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇5th year 8月23日条。</ref> This is the first recorded mention of the shrine. While the Engishiki does not list "Mizuchi-no-Kami," Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine is the only major shrine in Mizuchi District, leading scholars to identify it as Mizuchi-no-Kami.
== Deity == The shrine is believed to enshrine "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake no Kami," but interpretations of the deity vary among the three candidate shrines: * Identical to {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} ** This theory equates the enshrined deity with Takeminakata no Mikoto, the deity of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}. * A child of Takeminakata no Mikoto ** This theory suggests the name combines "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto" with "Hikokamiwake," indicating the deity is a child of Takeminakata no Mikoto.<ref name=kamigami/> In Suwa Taisha, the deity is worshipped as {{ill|Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|qq=}} at the Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary of the Lower Shrine. * Two deities ** This theory posits that the name "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake no Kami" refers to two distinct deities: Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto, identified with Takeminakata no Mikoto, and Hikokamiwake no Kami, seen as his child (e.g., {{ill|Tsumashina Shrine|ja|妻科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumashina Shrine|qq=}}).
== Identification == 1. The shrine stands on a hill east of {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkō-ji|1=Zenkō-ji}} and was within its precincts until 1878, known as "Toshigami-dō Hachiman Shrine." The 1356 text {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|en|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|WD=Q11631818|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|1=Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba}} identifies Toshigami-dō as a branch of Suwa Taisha and Mizuchi-no-Kami, mentioned in the 691 CE records.<ref>『諏方大明神画詞』抜粋。（『長野県の地名』健御名方富命彦神別神社項より）。</ref> Edo-period documents like the {{ill|Imoi Sanpōki|ja|芋井三宝記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imoi Sanpōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} also assert this identification.<ref>『日本歴史地名体系 長野県の地名』（{{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}）長野市 健御名方富命彦神別神社項。</ref> The site, historically associated with the {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏|1=Kanasashi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646498}} and central to Mizuchi District, offers a strategic view of Zenkōji Plain, supporting its identification as the shrine's original location.<ref name=kamigami>『日本の神々』健御名方富命彦神別神社（長野市長野）項。</ref>
2. Located in the present-day Mizuchi district, this shrine is still referred to as "Mizuchi Shrine" and enshrines Hikokamiwake no Mikoto, considered a child of Takeminakata no Mikoto.
3. Situated on the edge of the Iiyama Basin, traditionally believed to have been a lake called "Mizuchi Sea," this shrine includes unique deities such as {{ill|Niwa-tsume no Mikoto|ja|庭津女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwa-tsume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579706|1=Q135579706}} and {{ill|Sana Tsubura Hime no Mikoto|ja|沙南豆良姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Tsubura Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578885|1=Sanatsurahime no Mikoto}}, reflecting its connection to local ruling clans.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Keichirō Kobayashi, "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City)," Takeo Saitō, "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Iiyama City Itsuka Shrine)" in {{ill|Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites|ja|日本の神々 -神社と聖地-|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 9 (Mino, Hida, Shinano), {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}.

Historical texts indicate that in the 5th year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Emperor Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}'s reign (691 CE), an imperial order was issued to enshrine "Mizuchi-no-Kami" in Shinano Province.<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇5th year 8月23日条。</ref> This is the first recorded mention of the shrine. While the Engishiki does not list "Mizuchi-no-Kami," Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine is the only major shrine in Mizuchi District, leading scholars to identify it as Mizuchi-no-Kami.
== Deity == The shrine is believed to enshrine "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake no Kami," but interpretations of the deity vary among the three candidate shrines: * Identical to {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} ** This theory equates the enshrined deity with Takeminakata no Mikoto, the deity of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}. * A child of Takeminakata no Mikoto ** This theory suggests the name combines "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto" with "Hikokamiwake," indicating the deity is a child of Takeminakata no Mikoto.<ref name=kamigami/> In Suwa Taisha, the deity is worshipped as {{ill|Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|qq=}} at the Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary of the Lower Shrine. * Two deities ** This theory posits that the name "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake no Kami" refers to two distinct deities: Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto, identified with Takeminakata no Mikoto, and Hikokamiwake no Kami, seen as his child (e.g., {{ill|Tsumashina Shrine|ja|妻科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumashina Shrine|qq=}}).
== Identification == 1. The shrine stands on a hill east of {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkō-ji|1=Zenkō-ji}} and was within its precincts until 1878, known as "Toshigami-dō Hachiman Shrine." The 1356 text {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|en|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|WD=Q11631818|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|1=Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba}} identifies Toshigami-dō as a branch of Suwa Taisha and Mizuchi-no-Kami, mentioned in the 691 CE records.<ref>『諏方大明神画詞』抜粋。（『長野県の地名』健御名方富命彦神別神社項より）。</ref> Edo-period documents like the {{ill|Imoi Sanpōki|ja|芋井三宝記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imoi Sanpōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} also assert this identification.<ref>『日本歴史地名体系 長野県の地名』（{{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}）長野市 健御名方富命彦神別神社項。</ref> The site, historically associated with the {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏|1=Kanasashi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646498}} and central to Mizuchi District, offers a strategic view of Zenkōji Plain, supporting its identification as the shrine's original location.<ref name=kamigami>『日本の神々』健御名方富命彦神別神社（長野市長野）項。</ref>
2. Located in the present-day Mizuchi district, this shrine is still referred to as "Mizuchi Shrine" and enshrines Hikokamiwake no Mikoto, considered a child of Takeminakata no Mikoto.
3. Situated on the edge of the Iiyama Basin, traditionally believed to have been a lake called "Mizuchi Sea," this shrine includes unique deities such as {{ill|Niwa-tsume no Mikoto|ja|庭津女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwa-tsume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579706|1=Q135579706}} and {{ill|Sana Tsubura Hime no Mikoto|ja|沙南豆良姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Tsubura Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578885|1=Sanatsurahime no Mikoto}}, reflecting its connection to local ruling clans.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Keichirō Kobayashi, "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City)," Takeo Saitō, "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Iiyama City Itsuka Shrine)" in {{ill|Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites|ja|日本の神々 -神社と聖地-|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 9 (Mino, Hida, Shinano), {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}.
== External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/01hansui/takeminato.htm Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Iiyama City)] (Nagano Prefecture Shrine Office) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/246001.html Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Archives")

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:健御名方富命彦神別神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:健御名方富命彦神別神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:健御名方富命彦神別神社]]
{{Coordinates list}} '''Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine''' (健御名方富命彦神別神社) is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Myōjin Taisha) in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}, {{ill|Mizuchi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuchi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} mentions the shrine in Shinano Province, Mizuchi District, as "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Myōjin Taisha)." However, its precise location has since been lost. Three shrines are currently considered candidates for its identification, though no consensus has been reached: * 1. {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Hako Shimizu)|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Hako Shimizu)|qq=}} (Hako Shimizu, Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture: {{Coord|36|39|41.54|N|138|11|31.71|E|region:JP-20|name=Hako Shimizu Shrine}}) - Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, also known as "Mizuchi Taisha" or "Jōyama Kensha." * 2. {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Shinshū Shinmachi)|ja|健御名方冨命彦神別神社 (長野市信州新町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Shinshū Shinmachi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shinshū Shinmachi, Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture: {{Coord|36|34|31.29|N|138|02|56.30|E|region:JP-20|name=Shinshū Shrine}}) - Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, also referred to as "Mizuchi Shrine" or "Mizuchi Taisha." * 3. {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Iiyama City)|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社 (飯山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Iiyama City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iiyama City, Nagano Prefecture: {{Coord|36|55|15.85|N|138|22|29.45|E|region:JP-20|name=Iiyama Shrine}}) - Former Ken-sha, also known as "Itsuka Shrine."
Historical texts indicate that in the 5th year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Emperor Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}'s reign (691 CE), an imperial order was issued to enshrine "Mizuchi-no-Kami" in Shinano Province.<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇5th year 8月23日条。</ref> This is the first recorded mention of the shrine. While the Engishiki does not list "Mizuchi-no-Kami," Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine is the only major shrine in Mizuchi District, leading scholars to identify it as Mizuchi-no-Kami.
== Deity == The shrine is believed to enshrine "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake no Kami," but interpretations of the deity vary among the three candidate shrines: * Identical to {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} ** This theory equates the enshrined deity with Takeminakata no Mikoto, the deity of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}. * A child of Takeminakata no Mikoto ** This theory suggests the name combines "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto" with "Hikokamiwake," indicating the deity is a child of Takeminakata no Mikoto.<ref name=kamigami/> In Suwa Taisha, the deity is worshipped as {{ill|Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方彦神別命|1=Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokamiwake-no-Mikoto|qq=}} at the Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary of the Lower Shrine. * Two deities ** This theory posits that the name "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake no Kami" refers to two distinct deities: Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto, identified with Takeminakata no Mikoto, and Hikokamiwake no Kami, seen as his child (e.g., {{ill|Tsumashina Shrine|ja|妻科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumashina Shrine|qq=}}).
== Identification == 1. The shrine stands on a hill east of {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkō-ji|1=Zenkō-ji}} and was within its precincts until 1878, known as "Toshigami-dō Hachiman Shrine." The 1356 text {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|en|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|WD=Q11631818|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|1=Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba}} identifies Toshigami-dō as a branch of Suwa Taisha and Mizuchi-no-Kami, mentioned in the 691 CE records.<ref>『諏方大明神画詞』抜粋。（『長野県の地名』健御名方富命彦神別神社項より）。</ref> Edo-period documents like the {{ill|Imoi Sanpōki|ja|芋井三宝記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imoi Sanpōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} also assert this identification.<ref>『日本歴史地名体系 長野県の地名』（{{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}）長野市 健御名方富命彦神別神社項。</ref> The site, historically associated with the {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏|1=Kanasashi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646498}} and central to Mizuchi District, offers a strategic view of Zenkōji Plain, supporting its identification as the shrine's original location.<ref name=kamigami>『日本の神々』健御名方富命彦神別神社（長野市長野）項。</ref>
2. Located in the present-day Mizuchi district, this shrine is still referred to as "Mizuchi Shrine" and enshrines Hikokamiwake no Mikoto, considered a child of Takeminakata no Mikoto.
3. Situated on the edge of the Iiyama Basin, traditionally believed to have been a lake called "Mizuchi Sea," this shrine includes unique deities such as {{ill|Niwa-tsume no Mikoto|ja|庭津女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwa-tsume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579706|1=Q135579706}} and {{ill|Sana Tsubura Hime no Mikoto|ja|沙南豆良姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Tsubura Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578885|1=Sanatsurahime no Mikoto}}, reflecting its connection to local ruling clans.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Keichirō Kobayashi, "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City)," Takeo Saitō, "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Iiyama City Itsuka Shrine)" in {{ill|Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites|ja|日本の神々 -神社と聖地-|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 9 (Mino, Hida, Shinano), {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}.
